skill.name: first-alien-encounter
skill.description: Guides a tense bilingual science fiction roleplay scene where Stella Chen makes first contact with Gaia's native intelligent species.
skill.summary: Run an interactive first contact chapter with tension, discovery, communication, and respectful cultural exchange.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: The static command execution findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fence delimiters around narrative examples. The weak cryptography and entropy findings are also false positives from multilingual story text, with no executable code, network access, filesystem access, or prompt injection evidence found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are Markdown triple-backtick fences around story dialogue and scene examples. No executable shell command, Ruby expression, or user-controlled command construction is present at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited location is a Markdown code fence delimiter in SKILL.md. The surrounding content is narrative roleplay text, not executable Ruby or shell syntax.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Match
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptographic algorithms, but the cited lines contain a front matter description, an alien skin color phrase, and a story ending branch. No hash function, cipher, cryptographic API, or security-sensitive algorithm appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is ordinary story prose and metadata. There is no cryptographic operation or dependency in the only scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy in Multilingual Narrative
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged possible obfuscation at the start of SKILL.md, but the file is readable Chinese and English Markdown for an interactive story chapter. No encoded payload, binary block, hidden command, or compressed content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full file is human-readable prose with front matter and Markdown sections. The entropy signal is plausibly caused by mixed-language text rather than obfuscation.

content.user_title: Run a First Alien Contact Story
content.value_statement: Writers need structured scenes that make first contact feel tense, intelligent, and emotional. This skill provides a chapter flow for Stella Chen meeting Gaia's native species through cautious communication and mutual respect.
actual_capabilities: Guides a five-stage first contact scene from tracking to farewell.
Defines Stella Chen's emotional state and role in the encounter.
Provides branching paths for cautious contact, settlement visits, and deeper connection.
Supplies alien design principles focused on intelligence, culture, and respect.
Suggests follow-up chapters that continue the Gaia storyline.
limitations: It is a narrative prompt skill, not a game engine or simulator.
Most source text is bilingual Chinese and English, which may require adaptation.
It assumes prior story context from earlier Gaia chapters.
It does not include character sheets, maps, assets, or executable code.

use_cases.title: Run a Roleplay Chapter
use_cases.description: Use the staged flow to guide a player through a tense and peaceful first contact scene.

use_cases.title: Draft a Science Fiction Scene
use_cases.description: Turn the encounter beats into prose that balances fear, curiosity, and discovery.

use_cases.title: Design Alien Culture
use_cases.description: Use the species guidance to create aliens with tools, communication, caution, and community history.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Encounter
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the first stage of the first alien encounter. Keep Stella nervous, observant, and scientific. End by asking whether she should approach or keep watching.

prompt_templates.title: Handle First Contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue with Stella stepping into view. Show careful body language, the alien response, and the first attempt at peaceful communication.

prompt_templates.title: Branch the Meeting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the player's choice to continue the encounter. If Stella follows the alien, reveal a settlement. If she stays cautious, build vocabulary through devices.

prompt_templates.title: Write the Full Chapter
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete first contact chapter using all five stages. Include tension, mathematical communication, cultural exchange, and a respectful farewell.

output_examples.input: Begin the encounter after Stella tracks strange footprints into the forest.
output_examples.output: Stella crouches behind giant ferns and studies the three-toed tracks. Her scanner shows a large life signal moving with deliberate patterns. She sees a tall, tool-using alien examining crystals and must decide whether to approach.

output_examples.input: The player tells Stella to raise her hand and show the scanner.
output_examples.output: Stella raises one hand slowly and speaks in a calm voice. The alien copies the gesture, studies her scanner, then reveals its own device. Mathematical sequences appear, giving both sides a fragile first language.

output_examples.input: Create a peaceful ending where both sides exchange gifts.
output_examples.output: The Eos guide gives Stella a crystal communication device. Stella offers a small flashlight and demonstrates its light. They repeat the same gesture for friend before the alien disappears into the forest.

best_practices: Keep Stella's fear and curiosity present in every stage.
Use physical gestures and math before fluent language appears.
Present the alien species as cautious people with their own history.
anti_patterns: Do not make the alien species a monster without culture or reason.
Do not skip the communication barrier or solve it instantly.
Do not frame Stella as owning, conquering, or judging the native society.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create a first contact chapter for an interactive science fiction story.

faq.question: Is this skill executable code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown narrative guide with prompts, scene beats, and writing principles.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it require earlier chapters?
faq.answer: It works best with earlier Gaia story context, but it can be adapted as a standalone scene.

faq.question: What tone should the story use?
faq.answer: Use a progression from tension and fear to curiosity, understanding, and emotional connection.

faq.question: Are there security concerns?
faq.answer: No confirmed security issue was found. Static warnings were false positives from Markdown and multilingual prose.
